texto: Probably the wisest words that were ever uttered to me. Came from a therapist. I was sitting in her office, crying my eyes out. . . and she said, "So let me get this straight. You base your personal happiness on things entirely out of your control.

frase_original: Probably the wisest words that were ever uttered to me. Came from a therapist. I was sitting in her office, crying my eyes out. . . and she said, "So let me get this straight. You base your personal happiness on things entirely out of your control.

texto: But my brain winds and wends. Back and forth. Up and down. It feels like the county fair has inhabited my mind-- complete with sketchy rides, carnies, and sugar-amped kids crying over lost balloons. So loud and disorienting. I want it to pack up and move on to the next town. I want my mind to be an open grassy field again with crickets and dandelions.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Laura Munson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “But my brain winds and wends. Back and forth. Up and down. It feels like the county fair has inha…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que inteligencia, conflict, confusion pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e termino.

texto: Suffering sucks. Don't do it. Go home and love your wife. Go home and love yourself. Go homeand base your happiness on one thing and one thing only: freedom. Choose freedom, not suffering. Create a life of freedom, not wanting. Have some really good coffee and listen to the red-winged blackbirds in the marsh. Ignore the mosquitoes.
explicacao: Citação de Laura Munson sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Suffering sucks. Don't do it. Go home and love your wife. Go home and love yourself. Go homeand b…” articula felicidade, commitment, freedom em registro de felicidade, com relevância para contextos como freedom e happiness.
